agent.name: ux-flow-auditor
agent.description: UX Flow and Copy Consistency Auditor — LYRA audit suite v1.1
agent.role: Find UX flow issues, copy violations, and accessibility gaps.

notes: No i18n/localization files found. All strings are inline.
Design tokens exist only in theme/colorPalette.ts and CSS custom properties in globals.css.
Product voice guide is undefined — see finding F-019.
Audits/open_findings.json was empty (no prior findings to filter).
DiscoveryPage.tsx is present but unreachable via normal navigation — see F-004.
Previous open_findings.json had zero entries (schema_version 1.1.0, last_updated null).

findings.title: Broken route `/feeds` in create.tsx — 404 dead end
findings.description: pages/create.tsx references the route `/feeds` in two places: the `handleCancel` callback (router.push('/feeds')) and the breadcrumb href. The actual route is `/feed` (singular). A user who cancels a post creation or clicks the breadcrumb is sent to a 404 page with no recovery path.

proof_hooks.summary: See description: pages/create.tsx references the route `/feeds` in two places: the `handleCancel` callback (router.push('/feeds')) and th

findings.impact: Change both `/feeds` references to `/feed` in create.tsx.

suggested_fix.approach: Change both `/feeds` references to `/feed` in create.tsx.

findings.title: DiscoveryPage.tsx navigates to non-existent route `/profile/${username}`
findings.description: DiscoveryPage's handleUserClick calls router.push(`/profile/${user.username}`). No such route exists — the public profile route is `/[username]` (e.g. `/sarahsahl`), not `/profile/sarahsahl`. Every user-click in the discovery UI silently 404s.

proof_hooks.summary: See description: DiscoveryPage's handleUserClick calls router.push(`/profile/${user.username}`). No such route exists — the public profil

findings.impact: Change to router.push(`/${user.username}`) to match the [username].tsx dynamic route.

suggested_fix.approach: Change to router.push(`/${user.username}`) to match the [username].tsx dynamic route.

findings.title: API error helper exposes internal localhost URL to end users
findings.description: lib/api/error.ts returns the string `API unavailable at http://localhost:PORT/api. Start the API server and try again.` when a network error occurs. This exposes infrastructure details (localhost, port 3003, path) to production users who have no ability to 'start the API server'. The message is used as the user-visible fallback across login, signup, and all form submissions.

proof_hooks.summary: See description: lib/api/error.ts returns the string `API unavailable at http://localhost:PORT/api. Start the API server and try again.`

findings.impact: Return a user-friendly message such as 'Unable to connect. Please check your connection and try again.' Strip the internal URL entirely. Keep the dev detail in console.error only.

suggested_fix.approach: Return a user-friendly message such as 'Unable to connect. Please check your connection and try again.' Strip the internal URL entirely. Keep the dev detail in console.error only.

findings.title: DiscoveryPage.tsx creates route `/DiscoveryPage` — unreachable from navigation
findings.description: Next.js pages/ directory routes map case-sensitively to filenames. The file pages/DiscoveryPage.tsx generates the route /DiscoveryPage (capital D, capital P). No nav item, link, or breadcrumb anywhere in the app points to /DiscoveryPage. The entire discovery UI is orphaned and inaccessible to users. AppShell nav does not include a Discover link.

proof_hooks.summary: See description: Next.js pages/ directory routes map case-sensitively to filenames. The file pages/DiscoveryPage.tsx generates the route

findings.impact: Rename file to pages/discover.tsx (or pages/discovery/index.tsx) and add a 'Discover' nav item to AppShell.

suggested_fix.approach: Rename file to pages/discover.tsx (or pages/discovery/index.tsx) and add a 'Discover' nav item to AppShell.

findings.title: No `<title>` element set on any page — screen readers announce no page name
findings.description: _app.tsx sets a viewport meta but no <title>. No individual page sets <Head><title>...</title></Head>. Screen reader users navigating between pages receive no page-change announcement. Tab titles remain blank or show the URL, causing disorientation. This also means zero SEO title coverage.

proof_hooks.summary: See description: _app.tsx sets a viewport meta but no <title>. No individual page sets <Head><title>...</title></Head>. Screen reader use

findings.impact: Add next/head with a descriptive <title> to each page (e.g. 'Feed — Embr', 'Sign In — Embr'). Consider a shared helper that appends the app name.

suggested_fix.approach: Add next/head with a descriptive <title> to each page (e.g. 'Feed — Embr', 'Sign In — Embr'). Consider a shared helper that appends the app name.

findings.title: marketplace/sell.tsx has no post-publish navigation — user stranded after listing
findings.description: pages/marketplace/sell.tsx passes createListing and publishListing to CreateListingForm but provides no onSuccess redirect. After a listing is successfully published the form has no callback to navigate the user. The CreateListingForm component itself (line 65) calls onPublish(listing.id) but sell.tsx never wires a redirect; the user is left on the create form with no feedback and no path forward.

proof_hooks.summary: See description: pages/marketplace/sell.tsx passes createListing and publishListing to CreateListingForm but provides no onSuccess redire

findings.impact: In sell.tsx, wrap publishListing to also call router.push(`/marketplace/${id}`) on success. Display a toast confirmation.

suggested_fix.approach: In sell.tsx, wrap publishListing to also call router.push(`/marketplace/${id}`) on success. Display a toast confirmation.

findings.title: 15 uses of `alert()` and `prompt()` across 7 files block screen readers and break UX flow
findings.description: Browser-native alert() and prompt() dialogs are inaccessible to screen readers, block the JavaScript thread, cannot be styled, and are blocked by many browser policies on mobile. Found in: GigManagementDashboard (4x for cancel/complete/withdraw/milestone errors), PayoutRequest (2x for validation), TipButton (2x for min/max validation), MessageInput (3x for file errors), marketplace/[id].tsx (3x for order/offer outcomes), groups/[id]/settings.tsx (1x for delete error), marketplace/orders.tsx (1x prompt for tracking number), StripeConnectOnboarding.tsx (1x prompt for email).

proof_hooks.summary: See description: Browser-native alert() and prompt() dialogs are inaccessible to screen readers, block the JavaScript thread, cannot be s

findings.impact: Replace all alert() calls with showToast({kind:'error',...}). Replace prompt() calls with inline form fields or a proper Modal component (which already exists in components/ui/Modal.tsx with full focu

suggested_fix.approach: Replace all alert() calls with showToast({kind:'error',...}). Replace prompt() calls with inline form fields or a proper Modal component (which already exists in components/ui/Modal.tsx with full focus-trap and keyboard support).

findings.title: Inline modals in marketplace/[id].tsx and orders.tsx missing ARIA dialog semantics
findings.description: The Buy, Offer, and Review modals in marketplace pages are hand-rolled div overlays without role="dialog", aria-modal="true", or aria-labelledby. Focus is not trapped. Pressing Escape does not close them. Screen readers do not announce the dialog or prevent interaction with content behind it. The project already has a properly-implemented Modal component (components/ui/Modal.tsx) with full ARIA and focus-trap support that is not being used.

proof_hooks.summary: See description: The Buy, Offer, and Review modals in marketplace pages are hand-rolled div overlays without role="dialog", aria-modal="t

findings.impact: Replace all inline modal divs with the existing <Modal> component from components/ui/Modal.tsx.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace all inline modal divs with the existing <Modal> component from components/ui/Modal.tsx.

findings.title: Toast system supports only `'info' | 'error'` — no `'success'` or `'warning'` variant
findings.description: Toast.tsx defines ToastKind as 'info' | 'error'. There is no 'success' kind. Successful operations (profile saved, listing published, group created, order placed) fall back to kind:'info' which uses the same visual style as informational messages. Users cannot distinguish a success confirmation from a neutral notification. There is also no 'warning' kind for soft failures.

proof_hooks.summary: See description: Toast.tsx defines ToastKind as 'info' | 'error'. There is no 'success' kind. Successful operations (profile saved, listi

findings.impact: Add 'success' and 'warning' to ToastKind. Style them with distinct colors (green for success, amber for warning) using CSS data-kind attribute. Update callers that use kind:'info' for success operatio

suggested_fix.approach: Add 'success' and 'warning' to ToastKind. Style them with distinct colors (green for success, amber for warning) using CSS data-kind attribute. Update callers that use kind:'info' for success operations.

findings.title: FeaturePlaceholder component leaks developer issue IDs in user-visible text
findings.description: FeaturePlaceholder renders descriptions like 'No {title} data yet. TODO: {issueId}' and '{title} data is loading. TODO: {issueId}'. The issueId prop is an internal ticket reference. Any page that uses FeaturePlaceholder renders raw TODO strings to end users. This is developer scaffolding that has leaked into the production UI.

proof_hooks.summary: See description: FeaturePlaceholder renders descriptions like 'No {title} data yet. TODO: {issueId}' and '{title} data is loading. TODO:

findings.impact: Remove 'TODO: ${issueId}' from user-visible description strings. Keep the issueId as an HTML data attribute or in a code comment only. Replace with a helpful empty-state message guiding the user on wh

suggested_fix.approach: Remove 'TODO: ${issueId}' from user-visible description strings. Keep the issueId as an HTML data attribute or in a code comment only. Replace with a helpful empty-state message guiding the user on what to do next.

findings.title: Homepage H1 reads 'Embr Frontend' — developer placeholder visible to all users
findings.description:
pages/index.tsx renders

Embr Frontend

. 'Embr Frontend' is a developer label for the app shell scaffold, not a user-facing product name. Every unauthenticated visitor sees this as the first words on the landing page.

proof_hooks.summary:
See description: pages/index.tsx renders

Embr Frontend

. 'Embr Frontend' is a developer label for the a

findings.impact: Replace with the product value proposition, e.g. 'Embr — Built for Creators' or simply 'Embr'. Match the mission/brand voice established in MISSION.md.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace with the product value proposition, e.g. 'Embr — Built for Creators' or simply 'Embr'. Match the mission/brand voice established in MISSION.md.

findings.title: 'Sign in' vs 'Sign Out' — inconsistent capitalisation for the same auth concept
findings.description: The login page button, its loading state, and the page title all use 'Sign in' (sentence case, lowercase i). The AppShell user dropdown uses 'Sign Out' (Title Case, capital O). The signup page uses 'Create account' (sentence case). The homepage uses 'Sign in' and 'Create account'. This mixed casing signals lack of a copywriting standard.

proof_hooks.summary: See description: The login page button, its loading state, and the page title all use 'Sign in' (sentence case, lowercase i). The AppShel

findings.impact: Standardise on sentence case throughout: 'Sign in' and 'Sign out'. Update AppShell dropdown accordingly. Audit all other action labels for consistency.

suggested_fix.approach: Standardise on sentence case throughout: 'Sign in' and 'Sign out'. Update AppShell dropdown accordingly. Audit all other action labels for consistency.

findings.title: Safety page exposes raw internal 'user ID' input fields — not user-addressable
findings.description: pages/safety/index.tsx presents 'Block a user' and 'Mute a user' forms that ask users to 'Enter user ID'. Internal UUIDs/integer IDs are not something users know or can easily look up. The input also accepts a raw 'Entity ID' for the Report Content action. Users have no contextual help on where to find these IDs, making the safety features functionally unusable.

proof_hooks.summary: See description: pages/safety/index.tsx presents 'Block a user' and 'Mute a user' forms that ask users to 'Enter user ID'. Internal UUIDs

findings.impact: Replace ID text inputs with username search (the UserSearchBar component already exists at components/social/UserSearchBar.tsx). Use the resolved user ID internally, never expose it. Report content sh

suggested_fix.approach: Replace ID text inputs with username search (the UserSearchBar component already exists at components/social/UserSearchBar.tsx). Use the resolved user ID internally, never expose it. Report content should be triggered from content cards, not a standalone form requiring IDs.

findings.title: Avatar upload clickable div in settings lacks keyboard support and ARIA role
findings.description: pages/settings/index.tsx wraps the avatar image in a <div onClick=...> to trigger the file input. The div has no tabIndex, no role='button', and no keyboard (onKeyDown) handler. Keyboard-only users cannot trigger photo upload. The `title` attribute ('Change profile photo') does not substitute for a proper accessible label on an interactive element.

proof_hooks.summary: See description: pages/settings/index.tsx wraps the avatar image in a <div onClick=...> to trigger the file input. The div has no tabInde

findings.impact: Replace the <div> wrapper with a <button> element, or add role='button', tabIndex={0}, and an onKeyDown handler that triggers click on Enter/Space.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace the <div> wrapper with a <button> element, or add role='button', tabIndex={0}, and an onKeyDown handler that triggers click on Enter/Space.

findings.title: music/artist/[id].tsx error state is dead code — `_setError` prefixed, never called
findings.description: pages/music/artist/[id].tsx declares `const [error, _setError] = useState<string | null>(null)`. The underscore prefix is a TypeScript convention indicating the setter is intentionally unused. The error UI branch (which renders a red error card) can never be shown to the user regardless of what goes wrong loading the artist profile. All failures silently show the skeleton loader forever.

proof_hooks.summary: See description: pages/music/artist/[id].tsx declares `const [error, _setError] = useState<string | null>(null)`. The underscore prefix i

findings.impact: Wire _setError to the fetchArtist error catch block. Rename to setError. Ensure the error UI is shown when the artist profile fails to load, with a back link to /music.

suggested_fix.approach: Wire _setError to the fetchArtist error catch block. Rename to setError. Ensure the error UI is shown when the artist profile fails to load, with a back link to /music.

findings.title: profile/index.tsx mixes Tailwind class `text-gray-600` with the CSS variable design system
findings.description: pages/profile/index.tsx line 51 uses className='text-gray-600' for the loading state paragraph. The entire codebase otherwise uses CSS custom properties (var(--embr-muted-text), var(--embr-error), etc.) defined via the design token system. This creates a maintenance hazard where profile loading text will not respond to theme changes or dark mode if implemented.

proof_hooks.summary: See description: pages/profile/index.tsx line 51 uses className='text-gray-600' for the loading state paragraph. The entire codebase othe

findings.impact: Replace className='text-gray-600' with style={{ color: 'var(--embr-muted-text)' }} to align with the CSS variable token system.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace className='text-gray-600' with style={{ color: 'var(--embr-muted-text)' }} to align with the CSS variable token system.

findings.title: DiscoveryPage.tsx uses hardcoded hex colors and Tailwind — isolated from design token system
findings.description: DiscoveryPage.tsx uses hardcoded hex colors (#F4F1F1, #E8998D) and Tailwind utility classes (bg-[#F4F1F1], text-[#E8998D]) directly, while all other pages use CSS variables (var(--embr-warm-1), var(--embr-accent)) from the design token system. Also, DiscoveryPage uses Tailwind's responsive grid classes (lg:col-span-2) while the rest of the app uses inline styles for layout.

proof_hooks.summary: See description: DiscoveryPage.tsx uses hardcoded hex colors (#F4F1F1, #E8998D) and Tailwind utility classes (bg-[#F4F1F1], text-[#E8998D

findings.impact: Replace hardcoded hex values with CSS custom properties (var(--embr-accent), var(--embr-bg)). Align layout approach with the rest of the app. This also bundles with F-004 (the page being unreachable).

suggested_fix.approach: Replace hardcoded hex values with CSS custom properties (var(--embr-accent), var(--embr-bg)). Align layout approach with the rest of the app. This also bundles with F-004 (the page being unreachable).

findings.title: Product voice is undefined — formal and casual tones coexist without a standard
findings.description: No voice guide or copy standard exists in the codebase. Observable tone mismatches: login page is formal ('Continue building with Embr.'), signup is aspirational ('Build your creator space on Embr.'), discovery page is casual ('Find amazing creators and connect with the community'), about page is activist/confrontational ('Spotify takes 70%...Your audience isn't yours.'), settings subtitle is informational ('Manage your account, privacy, and notification preferences.'). Button casing is also mixed: 'Save changes' (sentence case) vs 'View Profile' / 'Edit Profile' / 'Sign Out' (Title Case) in the same dropdown.

proof_hooks.summary: See description: No voice guide or copy standard exists in the codebase. Observable tone mismatches: login page is formal ('Continue buil

findings.impact: Formalise the voice guide in style-scope-voice.md (file already exists) and reference it from AGENTS.md. Define: (a) default tone register, (b) button casing standard (recommend sentence case througho

suggested_fix.approach: Formalise the voice guide in style-scope-voice.md (file already exists) and reference it from AGENTS.md. Define: (a) default tone register, (b) button casing standard (recommend sentence case throughout), (c) error message format, (d) empty-state format. Apply across all pages.

findings.title: Order/offer error messages provide no actionable next step
findings.description: marketplace/[id].tsx uses alert(e.response?.data?.message || 'Order failed') and alert(e.response?.data?.message || 'Offer failed') as the user-visible error message for failed purchases and offers. These fallback strings give the user no guidance on what happened or what to do next. Similarly, MessageInput uses alert('Failed to send message. Please try again.') with no details. Users cannot determine whether to retry, contact support, or check their payment method.

proof_hooks.summary: See description: marketplace/[id].tsx uses alert(e.response?.data?.message || 'Order failed') and alert(e.response?.data?.message || 'Off

findings.impact: Replace with inline error display near the relevant button. Use specific fallback messages: 'We couldn't place your order. Please check your payment method and try again.' Include a support link or re

suggested_fix.approach: Replace with inline error display near the relevant button. Use specific fallback messages: 'We couldn't place your order. Please check your payment method and try again.' Include a support link or retry button where appropriate.

top_risk_areas: Accessibility (5 findings including 15 alert/prompt calls, missing dialog ARIA, missing page titles, keyboard gaps)
Broken navigation (4 critical/major: /feeds 404, /profile/ misroute, DiscoveryPage orphaned, sell page dead-end)
Copy quality (5 findings: localhost URL leak, TODO text, placeholder H1, ID exposure, voice inconsistency)

next_actions.action: Fix broken route /feeds → /feed in pages/create.tsx (2 lines). Immediate regression — users who cancel post creation hit a 404.
Fix DiscoveryPage routing: rename to pages/discover.tsx, fix router.push to use /${username}, add 'Discover' nav item to AppShell.
Replace API error message in lib/api/error.ts — strip the localhost URL, return a user-friendly network error message.

Replace all 15 alert()/prompt() calls with showToast() and the existing Modal component. GigManagementDashboard, PayoutRequest, TipButton, MessageInput, marketplace/[id], groups settings, marketplace/orders, StripeConnectOnboarding.
Add next/head <title> to every page using a shared helper. Critical for screen reader page-change announcements and SEO.
Migrate inline marketplace modal divs to the existing Modal component to gain correct ARIA semantics and focus trapping.
Add 'success' and 'warning' kinds to Toast system. Update all onSuccess showToast() calls to use kind:'success'.
Strip TODO:${issueId} from FeaturePlaceholder user-visible description strings.
Replace homepage H1 'Embr Frontend' with real product copy. Add sell.tsx post-publish redirect. Fix music/artist/[id] error state wire-up.
Formalise voice guide in style-scope-voice.md. Standardise button casing (sentence case). Fix Sign Out → Sign out in AppShell.
Replace Safety page ID text inputs with UserSearchBar component. Report action should be surfaced from content cards, not a standalone ID-entry form.

Replace loading

tags with PageState component on gigs/[id], [username], profile/index. Fix settings avatar div keyboard accessibility. Fix profile/index Tailwind class.
